A very important question of today's time uh can using AI in academic writing make you a better researcher or put your academic career at risk. Nowadays many people are using AI for their research assignments but only few actually understand the ethical consequences of this writing. Two recent events in academia they are enough to make us conscious about this thing. uh one is about the Hong Kong University professor who was stepped down from his position of associate dean just because he was the corresponding author of one of the article uh that contains some non-existent references that are generated by AI as that paper was originally written by one of his doctoral student the second event uh was about the retraction of an article that was published in science of total environment which is the elsewhere database journal and uh This article was redirected on the request of editor and chief who actually highlighted the 38 problematic references that do not exist in scientific literature and the authors were invited for to give the explanation. uh authorization just put their reasoning and and stating that it was just unintentional uh technical errors and they actually replaced those 38 problematic references with the 13 new references. On the verification editorial team they find that out of those 13 new references two were remain you know non-existent. So uh because of this editorial team actually lost the confidence about the findings that were presented in that article and finally they have decided to retract this article. I am not in against AI usage in academic writing. AI tools can be very helpful for brainstorming for language improvement and it can save your time. However, AI tools must be used ethically and wisely. No matter which AI tool you use but the final responsibility always lies with the author. It means that you must personally recheck every citation that the AI generates. Do not assume a reference is correct just because it is mentioned by AI. You should verify whether the article actually exists and whether the citation details are correct. Also, your responsibility does not end at first order judgment. You must go a step further and carefully read the original article just to confirm whether the text generated by AI truly matches what the author says. Using AI without verification can lead to incorrect citations, misinterpretations of the findings and can lead to serious academic integrity issues. So yes, use AI as a sporting tool but not as a replacement for your academic judgment.
